title: ulmus minor subsp. procera
intro: Ulmus minor subsp. procera, also known as the English elm, is a subspecies of the small-leaved elm tree native to Europe. The bark and leaves of the English elm have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Ulmus minor subsp. procera are believed to help with various health issues, including wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive problems, and it may also have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
